text:
There once was a small inventor named Herman who lived in a quaint little town. Herman was famous in town for his eccentric gadgets and devices. His workshop overflowed with gadgets in various stages of completion—some whirring and hissing, while others just sat staring blankly at walls.

One sunny weekend, Herman decided to try something new. He wanted to create a machine that could make every meal exactly as he imagined it. He gathered the finest ingredients, sprinkled in his proprietary blends, and poured in some of his secret gadgets for digestion—his digestion detector, a high-tech device that told his stomach exactly what it needed.*

"I now have the power to create the perfect meal," he proclaimed to the empty room.

His first creation involved steaks that were perfectly charred. He set them on a grill that only emitted the notes of a solo violin playing a tune that only steaks could appreciate. His soup was seasoned with mushroom extract and sea salt, so savory that no soup bowl was
